title: silene repens var. typica
intro: Silene repens var. typica, also known as creeping silene or creeping catchfly, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the Caryophyllaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral effects, derived from its alkaloids and flavonoids. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing fever, soothing digestive issues, and potentially alleviating respiratory problems, due to its expectorant and anti-spasmodic properties. The plant is typically prepared as a tincture, tea, or infusion, and is also used in herbal remedies, such as salves and ointments, to treat various skin conditions.
